title: Tender Turkish Malaysian Turkish Delight Biryani
description: A fatty and bouncy Malaysian dish featuring Turkish Delight, cooked to perfection in a papillote and served on a lazy-susan-spinner, this meal is perfect for a special occasion. It is also shojin-friendly, making it accessible to a wide range of diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the onion, garlic, and ginger into small pieces and saute them in ghee until they are softened.
Add the cumin seeds, coriander powder, and salt to the pan and stir for 1 minute.
Add the basmati rice to the pan and stir for 2 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water to the pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes.
Cut the Turkish Delight into small pieces and add it to the rice mixture.
Stir the mixture well and divide it into 4 equal portions.
Place each portion onto a sheet of papillote paper and fold the paper to create a packet.
Place the packets onto a lazy-susan-spinner and serve immediately.
